Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Show HN: Fio: 3D World editor/game engine – inspired by Radiant and Hammer 80 upvotes, 7 comments
  2. Chroma Context-1: Training a Self-Editing Search Agent 54 upvotes, 4 comments
  3. Whistler: Live eBPF Programming from the Common Lisp REPL 109 upvotes, 12 comments
  4. The European AllSky7 fireball network 73 upvotes, 7 comments
  5. Local Bernstein theory, and lower bounds for Lebesgue constants 17 upvotes, 0 comments
  6. OpenTelemetry profiles enters public alpha 174 upvotes, 27 comments
  7. Why so many control rooms were seafoam green (2025) 877 upvotes, 176 comments
  8. Show HN: Minimalist library to generate SVG views of scientific data 38 upvotes, 3 comments
  9. DOOM Over DNS 308 upvotes, 84 comments
  10. Show HN: I put an AI agent on a $7/month VPS with IRC as its transport layer 278 upvotes, 78 comments
  11. Generators in Lone Lisp 45 upvotes, 8 comments
  12. John Bradley, author of xv, has died 284 upvotes, 84 comments
  13. An unstoppable mushroom is tearing through North American forests 82 upvotes, 19 comments
  14. Running Tesla Model 3's computer on my desk using parts from crashed cars 905 upvotes, 313 comments
  15. QRV Operating System: QNX on RISC-V 21 upvotes, 3 comments
  16. My minute-by-minute response to the LiteLLM malware attack 389 upvotes, 148 comments
  17. HyperAgents: Self-referential self-improving agents 199 upvotes, 71 comments
  18. Agent-to-agent pair programming 85 upvotes, 28 comments
  19. Using FireWire on a Raspberry Pi 95 upvotes, 43 comments
  20. A Faster Alternative to Jq 197 upvotes, 104 comments
  21. $500 GPU outperforms Claude Sonnet on coding benchmarks 336 upvotes, 191 comments
  22. Anthropic Subprocessor Changes 89 upvotes, 43 comments
  23. Everything old is new again: memory optimization 71 upvotes, 34 comments
  24. We haven't seen the worst of what gambling and prediction markets will do 787 upvotes, 549 comments
  25. Colibri – chat platform built on the AT Protocol for communities big and small 125 upvotes, 81 comments
  26. The Legibility of Serif and Sans Serif Typefaces (2022) 55 upvotes, 31 comments
  27. Hold on to Your Hardware 224 upvotes, 160 comments
  28. Apple discontinues the Mac Pro 456 upvotes, 364 comments
  29. Schedule tasks on the web 184 upvotes, 142 comments
  30. We rewrote JSONata with AI in a day, saved $500k/year 195 upvotes, 184 comments

Last generated 9:00pm, 27 March 2026. Source here.
